summary: The CWD-relative source lookup blocker for secp256k1_extended.cl is RESOLVED. OpenCLBackend::ensure_extended_kernels() now uses a multi-strategy resolver (resolve_opencl_kernel) that searches: (1) UFSECP_OPENCL_KERNEL_DIR env var, (2) executable-relative paths via /proc/self/exe, (3) upward walk from exe dir looking for src/opencl/kernels/, (4) legacy CWD-relative paths. Both bench_lbtc_workloads and bench_lbtc_public_ops now reach the OpenCL RTX 5060 Ti path from repo-root and build-root invocations without manual file copying.

root_cause.reason: The extended kernel loader checked only CWD-relative paths. The libbitcoin benchmark binaries invoked from repo root or build root did not reach src/opencl/kernels/secp256k1_extended.cl.

resolution.strategy: Added resolve_opencl_kernel() helper with 7-strategy search: env var, exe-relative (8 depth variants), upward walk (12 levels), legacy CWD-relative fallback. Improved error message to list all candidates searched.

validation.workload_benchmark: bench_lbtc_workloads --batch-class small --iters 3 → all 4 workloads emit GPU rows (backend=opencl, device=NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti). All 8 JSON rows pass check_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py.
validation.public_ops_benchmark: bench_lbtc_public_ops → 22 rows total: 11 CPU-forced api_correctness rows and 11 direct-production gpu_acceleration rows (backend=opencl, device=NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti). All 22 rows pass check_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py.

remaining_blocker.note: CWD-relative source lookup blocker is fixed. The resolver now finds secp256k1_extended.cl from any invocation directory.
